title: Writing - Part 1: Volunteering
description: Your class has been discussing the benefits of young people doing volunteer work.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the benefits in your notes. You should explain which benefit you think is most valuable, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Volunteering looks impressive on a CV and helps find employment.
Helping others gives a sense of purpose and satisfaction.
Young people learn more from real experience than from books.

structure.introduction: Introduce volunteering and the two benefits you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first benefit (e.g., helping the community) and its impact.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second benefit (e.g., developing personal skills) and how it helps young people.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both benefits and state which you think is most valuable.

content.modelAnswer:
Volunteering is an activity that benefits both the individual and society as a whole. Giving up free time to help others is a commendable act that millions of young people around the world engage in each year. This essay will discuss the benefits of helping the community and developing personal skills through volunteering.

Working as a volunteer makes a real and meaningful difference to local communities. From helping at food banks to participating in environmental clean-up projects, volunteers address needs that might otherwise go unmet. Knowing that your efforts are improving the lives of others gives a powerful sense of purpose and satisfaction.

Additionally, volunteering allows young people to develop valuable skills such as communication, teamwork and problem-solving. These abilities are highly prized by employers and are often difficult to acquire in a classroom setting. Young volunteers also gain confidence and a broader perspective on the world around them.

In conclusion, while community impact is clearly important, I believe the personal development gained through volunteering is its most valuable aspect. The skills and experiences young people acquire not only benefit them immediately but also help shape them into more capable and empathetic adults in the future.
